title: The Animal Too Fearless to Have Predators
script: There is an animal so fearless that lions, leopards, and cobras mostly just leave it alone, and it is only the size of a small dog. The honey badger. It will fight animals ten times its size, shrug off a venomous snake bite, and then go right back to eating the snake that bit it. Its skin is so thick and loose that it can twist around inside an attacker's grip and bite back. It raids beehives, digs up cobras, and steals kills from far bigger predators. Pound for pound, it might be the toughest animal alive. So where does that fearlessness even come from?
